MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Josette D. Johnson, Esq. of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party Nibco Inc.
Anne T. Acuna, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Johnny Lee Fuller

RULING:
Defendant Nibco Inc.'s motion for summary judgment. No argument was made and the tentative ruling was adopted: Defendant's motion is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication is off calendar because plaintiff has dismissed his claims for false representation and punitive damages. Due to plaintiff's deposition testimony, defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess evidence that he was exposed to original packing in Nibco valves. Liberally construed, plaintiff's deposition testimony is sufficient to preclude defendant's reliance on the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. The evidence submitted with defendant's reply papers was not considered because plaintiff was not provided an opportunity to respond to that evidence. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
